title: Robiquetia tierneyana
taxonomy: Robiquetia tierneyana is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family consists of approximately 30,000 species, making it the second-largest flowering plant family. At the genus level, Robiquetia tierneyana is part of the subtribe Gastrodieae. Within the Orchidaceae family, the Gastrodieae subtribe is one of the 15 subtribes that are classified within the tribe Orchideae. The exact classification of Robiquetia tierneyana is based on its morphological and genetic characteristics.

intro: Robiquetia tierneyana is an epiphytic orchid species native to Papua New Guinea, known for its unique floral and foliage characteristics. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to exhibit potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and infection prevention. The bioactive constituents of Robiquetia tierneyana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Robiquetia tierneyana is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
